clusterWarnings: 3+ negation KILLs recur across the harbor/departure stretch, the mid-crossing stretch, and the landfall stretch.
Fragment-pivot rhythm leaks across narration and dialogue.

findings.line: No rescue hidden in it. No second thought from Dalaran or Silvermoon. Just facts.
findings.reason: Pattern 7 chained negation building to a simple positive statement.

findings.line: Not because she meant to draw it. The temptation had burned out somewhere between Stratholme and the coast.
findings.deletionTest: The temptation had burned out somewhere between Stratholme and the coast.
findings.reason: Pattern 3 source-swap pivot refusing the real cause before stating it.
findings.suggestedFix: The temptation had burned out somewhere between Stratholme and the coast.

findings.line: No spell. No last attempt. Just the amber weight of it in her palm, dull as old resin.
findings.deletionTest: Just the amber weight of it in her palm, dull as old resin.
findings.reason: Pattern 8 mundane reveal chain delaying the actual image.
findings.suggestedFix: The amber weight of it lay in her palm, dull as old resin.

findings.reason: Pattern 2 fragment pivot withholding the committed image for a fake reveal.

findings.line: No blind reaching. No force for its own sake.
findings.reason: Pattern 7 chained negation scaffolding before the real action sentence that follows.
Pattern 2 fragment pivot using a negated first beat as dead scaffolding.

findings.line: "Not a mage's some." "A real some, then."
findings.reason: Pattern 2 dialogue pivot refusing the usable phrasing before naming it.

findings.line: No gentleness in it. Just a hard drag into the lee of the stern ladder, where a body could go down without being trampled.
findings.deletionTest: Just a hard drag into the lee of the stern ladder, where a body could go down without being trampled.
findings.reason: Pattern 2 fragment pivot clearing its throat before the concrete action.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas dragged her into the lee of the stern ladder, where a body could go down without being trampled.

findings.deletionTest: Burn pattern old enough to have taken weather.
findings.reason: Pattern 7 stacked timing negations restating information the previous sentence already carries.

findings.line: Arthas had not merely passed through. He had been here long enough to cut dock, land stores, and move inland.
findings.deletionTest: He had been here long enough to cut dock, land stores, and move inland.
findings.reason: Pattern 5 preamble negation priming a smaller truth before the actual claim.
findings.suggestedFix: He had been here long enough to cut dock, land stores, and move inland.

clusterWarnings: Dedicated P5/P6 second sweep completed across the whole chapter.
Harbor/departure and open-water sections each carry multiple agency-laundering hits.

findings.line: Sea years lived in the way she planted her boots.
findings.subject: Sea years
findings.realAgent: Dagny's practiced stance and boot placement after long work at sea
findings.reason: Abstract history is carrying bodily presence instead of the prose naming the visible effect.
findings.suggestedFix: She planted her boots with the certainty of long years at sea.

findings.line: The sort institutions could still carry after the power to alter anything had failed.
findings.subject: institutions
findings.realAgent: relay traffic and runners still passing route facts hand to hand
findings.reason: The sentence launders human relay mechanics onto an abstraction.
findings.suggestedFix: The sort of facts that still passed hand to hand after the power to alter anything had failed.

findings.line: The whole vessel answered the water in small muscular adjustments her knees resented on principle.
findings.subject: vessel
findings.realAgent: the hull and rigging flexing under the water's motion
findings.reason: The prose gives the ship a conversational verb instead of naming its physical response.
findings.suggestedFix: The whole vessel flexed with the water in small muscular adjustments her knees resented on principle.

findings.line: The net yard gave back a foot of distance, then another.
findings.subject: net yard
findings.realAgent: the schooner pulling away and opening the gap from shore
findings.reason: Second-sweep geography hit: scenery is made to perform the ship's movement.
findings.suggestedFix: The distance to the net yard opened by a foot, then another.

findings.subject: North
findings.realAgent: the supernatural headwind and the source's resistance
findings.reason: Second-sweep abstract-direction hit: the prose assigns active resistance to a direction instead of the hostile force in the scene.

findings.line: The sea had kept it distant all morning by occupying every other sense.
findings.subject: sea
findings.realAgent: cold, motion, nausea, and deck labor swamping Jaina's attention
findings.reason: The environment is made to manage attention instead of the prose naming the concrete sensory overload.
findings.suggestedFix: Cold, motion, and deck labor had kept it distant all morning.

audits.cleanReason: Counted all seven tic families. The chapter has one non-said performance tag, one physical tightened, one eased, and one generic gaze drop under the relevant caps; no tic category exceeds its chapter limit.

summary.verdictReason: Fail: 16 mild KILLs remain across negation-addiction-hunting-guide.md and intent-smear-agency-laundering-guide.md. recurring-prose-tics.md stayed under cap, but the mild total is well above the pass threshold.
